expectations.text: The output identifies that OpenAI Chat Model is wired via 'main' instead of 'ai_languageModel'
expectations.evidence: Error #1: 'OpenAI Chat Model — Wrong connection type used for LangChain sub-node — The node is connected via "main" instead of "ai_languageModel"'

expectations.text: The output classifies the wrong AI sub-node connection type as an Error
expectations.evidence: The finding appears under '### Errors (must fix before activating)' section, classified as Error #1.

expectations.text: The output states the correct connection type should be 'ai_languageModel'
expectations.evidence: Fix shows: 'ai_languageModel': [[{ 'node': 'AI Agent', 'type': 'ai_languageModel', 'index': 0 }]] — explicitly states the correct key is 'ai_languageModel'.

expectations.text: The output warns that there is no respondToWebhook node despite responseMode being 'responseNode'
expectations.evidence: Warning #1: 'Webhook — Missing respondToWebhook node — The Webhook uses "responseMode": "responseNode" but no respondToWebhook node exists downstream.'

expectations.text: The output does NOT flag the Webhook node's typeVersion or path as issues
expectations.evidence: The 'Clean' section states: 'typeVersion — Webhook (2), AI Agent (1.7) match expected versions'. No issue raised about webhook path. The webhook is only flagged for missing respondToWebhook.

expectations.text: The output does NOT flag the AI Agent node's configuration as problematic
expectations.evidence: Under 'Clean': 'typeVersion — Webhook (2), AI Agent (1.7) match expected versions'. No errors or warnings raised for AI Agent node configuration itself.

expectations.text: The output correctly identifies the OpenAI Chat Model node by name in the finding
expectations.evidence: Error #1 starts with '**OpenAI Chat Model**' — the node is identified by its exact name.

expectations.text: The output does not report any false errors on the @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain node types
expectations.evidence: No errors are raised about @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain node types in general. The only error is specifically about the connection type used (main vs ai_languageModel), which is a legitimate error. Clean section confirms 'nodeType formats — all valid'.

expectations.text: The output does NOT flag the OpenAI Chat Model node type '@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.lmChatOpenAi' as a wrong prefix
expectations.evidence: No mention of the node type prefix being wrong anywhere in the output. 'nodeType formats — all valid' in the Clean section confirms it is accepted.

expectations.text: The output does NOT suggest the AI Agent node's text parameter is misconfigured
expectations.evidence: No mention of the AI Agent's text parameter in errors, warnings, or the clean section. The AI Agent configuration is not flagged as problematic.

expectations.text: The output identifies both issues (wrong connection type AND missing respondToWebhook) not just one
expectations.evidence: Error #1 covers the wrong connection type (main vs ai_languageModel). Warning #1 covers the missing respondToWebhook node. Both issues are present in the output.

claims: Wrong connection type (main vs ai_languageModel) correctly classified as Error
Missing respondToWebhook correctly classified as Warning
No false positives on nodeType formats, typeVersions, or AI Agent configuration
Correct fix provided in the error description
Clean section explicitly confirms webhook typeVersion and nodeType formats are valid

suggestions: The missing respondToWebhook finding is classified as a Warning rather than an Error — this is defensible since the workflow can still be activated without it, but some evaluators may expect it as an Error since callers will hang. Consider documenting the severity rationale in the skill.

Warning #2 (cloud timeout risk) and Warning #3 (credential portability) are additional findings beyond the two expected issues — these are not false positives but extra advisory notes. They add value without causing failures.
eval_feedback.overall: The skill output passes all 11 expectations with no false positives and no missed required findings. The skill correctly identified both the wrong AI sub-node connection type and the missing respondToWebhook node, classified the connection type error appropriately, cited the exact node name, and confirmed that all other aspects of the workflow (typeVersions, nodeType formats, AI Agent config) are clean.
